title: fix(secrets): point local dev workers at staging tinybird workspace
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Third leg of the staging-workspace isolation work (after #11128 and #11135). Today, running `npm run dev` in enter or gen writes events to the **prod** Tinybird workspace because:

- `enter.pollinations.ai/secrets/dev.vars.json` has `TINYBIRD_INGEST_TOKEN` and `TINYBIRD_READ_TOKEN` pointing at prod
- `gen.pollinations.ai/secrets/dev.vars.json` has `TINYBIRD_INGEST_TOKEN` pointing at prod

Local dev events get tagged `environment="local"` but still land in prod tables.

This swaps those tokens to the staging workspace, so local dev pollutes staging instead of prod. `TINYBIRD_SYNC_TOKEN` left unchanged — sync isn't exercised during local dev, and a staging sync token doesn't exist yet (tracked in #11127).

Related: #11128, #11135, #11127

gist.headline: Keeping the hive clean
gist.blurb: We've updated our local development environments to point to staging analytics instead of production. No more test data mixing with the real honey!
gist.summary: Local development environments for the enter and gen workers now write events to the staging Tinybird workspace instead of production.
gist.impact: Developers running local workers will no longer pollute production analytics tables with test events.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee sorting data seeds into a 'STAGING' bin, keeping them separate from the main 'PROD' garden. The monitor robot helping clean up.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot sorting glowing data seeds into a small wooden box labeled 'STAGING', keeping them safely away from a larger 'PROD' garden bed. A chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head helps by holding a tiny broom. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
